disclaimer: Reconnecting terminology is publicly shared guidance designed to help people approach ancestry investigation with accuracy and humility. This is not tribal enrollment guidance.

protocolPhrase.verbatim: I am of Coast Salish ancestry through my biological line, but I was separated by adoption and am now seeking to learn the history of my ancestors.
protocolPhrase.usageContext: Use this phrasing (or adapt to your specific situation) when approaching cultural centers, elders, or community events. It identifies your relationship honestly without making claims you cannot verify.
protocolPhrase.pretendianNote: The Pretendian concern is real and impacts Indigenous communities. Honesty about the limits of your knowledge — including saying 'I don't know yet' — is the correct approach. Fabricated or exaggerated ancestry claims cause direct harm to nations whose membership criteria are being misrepresented.

terms.definition: A person with Indigenous ancestry who was separated from their community — through adoption, residential school, Sixties Scoop, or other disruptions — and is now seeking cultural connection.
terms.usageGuidance: Self-identifying term used by community members and organizations working in this space. Use only as a descriptor for someone who self-identifies this way.
terms.culturalSensitivity: This identity involves grief, resilience, and ongoing community negotiation. Do not assume ease or resolution.

terms.definition: The mass removal of Indigenous children from their families into non-Indigenous foster care and adoption, predominantly in the 1960s-1980s across Canada.
terms.usageGuidance: Historical term for a specific policy period. Use when describing the context of family separation.
terms.culturalSensitivity: Many reconnecting descendants are Sixties Scoop survivors or their children. The term describes a specific injustice, not a neutral adoption practice.

terms.definition: The concept that cultural knowledge, connection, and identity persist through ancestral lineage even when cultural transmission was interrupted.
terms.usageGuidance: Acknowledge the concept respectfully. Do not claim mechanism or make promises about what it means for a specific person. This is a meaningful concept held by many Indigenous people — it is not a pseudoscientific claim.

terms.definition: The process of tracing ancestry through geographic and family records to determine whether one's heritage is with Saltwater People (coastal) or River/Mountain People (Cascade/Plateau).
terms.usageGuidance: This is the Salish Sea Ways approach to ancestry research. It provides geographic framing without requiring tribal enrollment documentation to begin learning.
terms.culturalSensitivity: Watershed investigation is a starting point, not a conclusion. Many families have both coastal and interior connections.

terms.definition: Formal membership in a federally recognized tribe or Canadian First Nation, determined by each nation's own citizenship criteria.
terms.usageGuidance: Do not suggest that learning heritage content requires enrollment. Do not suggest that enrollment is the only valid form of cultural connection. Enrollment criteria are each nation's sovereign right to determine.
terms.culturalSensitivity: The pack's position: you do not need a tribal ID to begin learning. Formal community connection and enrollment are separate questions.

resourcesForReconnecting.name: Sixties Scoop Network
resourcesForReconnecting.description: National support network for Sixties Scoop survivors and families.

resourcesForReconnecting.name: National Indian Child Welfare Association (NICWA)
resourcesForReconnecting.description: US-based organization focused on tribal child welfare and family preservation.

resourcesForReconnecting.name: U'mista Cultural Centre (Alert Bay)
resourcesForReconnecting.description: Kwakwaka'wakw cultural center and repatriated potlatch collection — publicly accessible for learning.

resourcesForReconnecting.name: Tulalip Tribes Cultural Resources
resourcesForReconnecting.description: Tulalip Tribes official website including cultural heritage resources.

resourcesForReconnecting.name: Squamish Nation
resourcesForReconnecting.description: Squamish Nation official website with language and cultural learning resources.
